steps.title: Identify Farmer's contribution
steps.content: The text explains that older histories relied too heavily on ideological works, which overrepresented a small group of mostly male thinkers. Farmer broadened the primary sources and produced a better understanding of the movement and Black women's roles.

steps.title: Match the main idea
steps.content:
• Choice A: Summarizes Farmer's method and its improved historical understanding.
  • Choice B: Says historians ignored intellectual dimensions, but they relied on ideological works.
  • Choice C: Introduces criticism that the text never mentions.
  • Choice D: Speculates about movement figures' views, not the text's main idea.
The answer is A.
